name: SIG Lite
description: Shared Assessments SIG Lite — standardised third-party security risk questionnaire covering 18 domains

questions.text: Does your organisation have a formal Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) program?
questions.follow_up: Please describe the governance structure and frequency of risk review.

questions.text: Is your ERM program reviewed and approved by executive leadership or the board at least annually?
Do you conduct a formal risk assessment of information security risks at least annually?
questions.follow_up: What methodology or framework (e.g. ISO 31000, NIST RMF) is used?

questions.text: Do you maintain a risk register that is reviewed and updated on a defined schedule?
Does your organisation have a documented Information Security Policy that has been approved by management?
How frequently is your Information Security Policy reviewed and updated?
Is your security policy communicated to all employees and relevant contractors?
questions.follow_up: Describe the communication and acknowledgement mechanism used.

questions.text: Do you have a documented acceptable use policy covering corporate systems, data, and internet access?
Is there a designated individual or team responsible for information security (e.g. CISO, Security Officer)?
questions.follow_up: Please provide the role title and reporting line.

questions.text: Are information security responsibilities defined and communicated within role descriptions or job profiles?
Does your organisation have a security awareness training programme for all personnel?
questions.follow_up: Describe training frequency, delivery method, and tracking of completion.

questions.text: Do you conduct security assessments or audits of your own organisation at least annually (e.g. internal audit, external pen test)?
Do you maintain a formal inventory of information assets (hardware, software, and data)?
Are information assets classified according to a defined data classification scheme?
questions.follow_up: Describe the classification tiers and how they are enforced.

questions.text: Do you have documented procedures for the secure disposal of physical and electronic media?
Are data handling and retention policies defined and enforced for each data classification tier?
Do you perform background checks on employees prior to employment (or within a defined onboarding window)?
questions.follow_up: Describe the scope of checks performed and any role-based variations.

questions.text: Do employees sign confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements upon hire?
Is there a documented offboarding process that includes timely revocation of system access?
questions.follow_up: What is the target SLA for access revocation after termination?

questions.text: Do you conduct role-specific security training for personnel with elevated access or sensitive responsibilities?
Do you operate physical data centres or server rooms under your direct control?
questions.follow_up: Describe physical access controls, surveillance, and environmental monitoring in place.

questions.text: Are physical access rights to secure areas reviewed and updated at defined intervals?
Are environmental controls (HVAC, fire suppression, flood detection) in place and monitored for your primary processing facilities?
Is physical access to critical areas logged and reviewed?
Are change management procedures in place and followed for changes to production systems?
questions.follow_up: Describe your change advisory board or approval workflow.

questions.text: Do you conduct capacity planning to ensure availability of systems supporting your service?
Are production and non-production (development/test) environments separated?
Do you have a documented and tested backup and recovery procedure for critical systems and data?
questions.follow_up: Describe backup frequency, offsite/cloud replication, and recovery time objective (RTO).

questions.text: Is access to systems and data provisioned using the principle of least privilege?
Is multi-factor authentication (MFA) enforced for remote access and privileged account access?
questions.follow_up: Which MFA mechanisms are used (TOTP, hardware key, push notification) and for which access paths?

questions.text: Are user access rights reviewed and recertified at least annually?
questions.follow_up: Describe the recertification process and how exceptions are handled.

questions.text: Are privileged accounts (admin, root, service accounts) managed separately with enhanced controls?
Do you use a Privileged Access Management (PAM) or password vault solution?
questions.follow_up: Identify the solution and describe how credentials are rotated.

questions.text: Do you follow a secure software development lifecycle (SSDLC) that includes security requirements and code review?
questions.follow_up: Describe the key security gates and whether they are automated or manual.

questions.text: Are your applications subject to regular penetration testing by qualified internal or third-party testers?
questions.follow_up: Describe frequency, scope, and how findings are tracked to remediation.

questions.text: Do you perform automated static (SAST) or dynamic (DAST) application security testing as part of your CI/CD pipeline?
Do you have a documented vulnerability disclosure or bug bounty programme?
Do you have a documented and tested Incident Response Plan (IRP)?
questions.follow_up: When was the IRP last tested (tabletop exercise or live drill)?

questions.text: Do you have a defined process for notifying affected customers of a security breach within a specified timeframe?
questions.follow_up: What is your committed notification timeframe and channel?

questions.text: Have you experienced a material security incident in the past 24 months that affected customer data?
questions.follow_up: Describe the nature of the incident, impact, and remediation taken.

questions.text: Do you maintain and review security incident logs for at least 12 months?
Do you have a documented Business Continuity Plan (BCP) that covers your core service delivery?
questions.follow_up: Describe your recovery time objective (RTO) and recovery point objective (RPO) for critical systems.

questions.text: Is the BCP tested at least annually?
questions.follow_up: Describe the test type (tabletop, simulation, full failover) and date of last test.

questions.text: Do you have a Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP) for your critical IT systems with documented RTOs and RPOs?
Do you have redundant infrastructure or failover capabilities to maintain service availability?
questions.follow_up: Describe geographic distribution and failover mechanism.

questions.text: Which of the following certifications or attestations does your organisation currently hold? Select all that apply.
Are you subject to any regulatory data protection obligations (e.g. GDPR, CCPA) relevant to the data you process on our behalf?
questions.follow_up: List the applicable regulations and describe your compliance programme.

questions.text: Have you been subject to any regulatory enforcement actions, fines, or material findings in the past 3 years?
questions.follow_up: Describe the finding, regulator, and outcome.

questions.text: Do you have a compliance monitoring programme that tracks adherence to applicable laws, regulations, and contractual obligations?

domains.name: End User Device and Mobile Security

questions.text: Is a Mobile Device Management (MDM) or Unified Endpoint Management (UEM) solution used for corporate devices?
questions.follow_up: Identify the solution and describe key enforced policies (encryption, PIN, remote wipe).

questions.text: Is full-disk encryption enforced on all laptops and mobile devices used to access corporate data?
Do you have a documented Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) policy that defines security requirements for personal devices accessing corporate systems?
Is endpoint detection and response (EDR) or antivirus software deployed and maintained on all end-user devices?
Is network access segmented using firewalls, VLANs, or micro-segmentation to limit lateral movement?
questions.follow_up: Describe the segmentation architecture for production environments.

questions.text: Do you employ intrusion detection or prevention systems (IDS/IPS) on your network perimeter and/or internally?
Is remote access to your network restricted to authorised personnel and secured via VPN or Zero Trust architecture?
questions.follow_up: Describe the remote access mechanism and MFA requirements.

questions.text: Are firewall rule sets reviewed at a defined frequency to remove unnecessary rules?
Do you maintain a publicly available privacy notice or policy that describes how personal data is collected, used, and shared?
Do you have documented procedures to handle data subject access, deletion, and correction requests within regulatory timeframes?
questions.follow_up: What is your target response time and how are requests tracked?

questions.text: Do you have a Data Protection Officer (DPO) or equivalent privacy role?
Do you conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) for high-risk processing activities?
Do you use any sub-processors or third parties to process personal data on your customers' behalf?
questions.follow_up: Provide a list of sub-processors and describe your sub-processor oversight programme.

questions.text: Do you have a vulnerability management programme that scans infrastructure and applications on a regular schedule?
questions.follow_up: Describe scan frequency and your SLA for remediating critical and high vulnerabilities.

questions.text: Do you subscribe to threat intelligence feeds or information sharing communities (e.g. ISACs) relevant to your industry?
Do you conduct threat modelling on critical systems or new features prior to deployment?
Have you deployed anti-phishing and email security controls (e.g. SPF, DKIM, DMARC, email gateway filtering)?
Are server hardening standards applied to all production servers (physical and virtual)?
questions.follow_up: Identify the baseline used (e.g. CIS Benchmarks, DISA STIGs) and how compliance is verified.

questions.text: Is patch management performed for operating systems and server software within defined SLAs based on criticality?
questions.follow_up: What are your patching SLAs for critical, high, and medium severity patches?

questions.text: Is host-based intrusion detection or file integrity monitoring deployed on production servers?
Do you use configuration management tooling (e.g. Ansible, Chef, Puppet, Terraform) to enforce server configurations as code?
Is data encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher for all external-facing services?
Is sensitive data encrypted at rest using industry-standard algorithms (e.g. AES-256)?
questions.follow_up: Describe which data stores are encrypted and the encryption mechanism.

questions.text: Do you have a documented key management policy that covers key generation, rotation, storage, and destruction?
questions.follow_up: Describe key rotation frequency and the key management system used.

questions.text: Are cryptographic keys stored separately from the data they protect, using a dedicated key management service or HSM?
Are deprecated or weak cryptographic algorithms (e.g. MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4) prohibited in your systems?
questions.follow_up: Please describe which legacy algorithms remain in use and your remediation timeline.
